label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to work through this step by step. So the question is asking what Mr. Williams was doing when he was stopped by the policeman. The options are A to D. First, let me recap the story. Mr. Williams comes to a crossroads with a "SLOW" sign. He slows down, looks both ways, and then drives across without stopping completely. Then a policeman stops him and says he didn't stop. Mr. Williams responds that the sign only says SLOW, not STOP. The policeman then realizes he's in the wrong street. The key here is the sequence of actions. The sign says SLOW. Mr. Williams slows down but doesn't stop. According to the story, at crossroads with a SLOW sign, drivers don't have to stop unless there's something coming. So he slowed down (which he did) and then drove across without stopping. So when the policeman stopped him, what was he doing? The policeman says he didn't stop at the crossing. Mr. Williams says he did go slow. The policeman then checks and realizes the sign is SLOW, but then the officer says he's in the wrong street. So maybe the officer was under the impression that the sign was a STOP sign and was watching the wrong road. Now, the question is about what Mr. Williams was doing when he was stopped. The options are: A. High speed - but he slowed down, so probably not. B. Driving onto the main road - Well, the story says he came to the main road. When he got to the main road, he slowed. But the exact timing is when he was on the main road. The sign says to stop if it's a STOP sign, but he was on the main road. Wait, the first paragraph says that if the sign is SLOW, they don't have to stop unless something's coming. So he was on the main road, or was he entering the main road? The description says he came to a crossroad with a SLOW sign. So he was approaching the main road. The sign is there to tell him to slow down when he comes to the main road. So he slowed down when he came to the main road and then drove across (presumably onto the main road). So when the policeman stopped him, he was on the main road. But the question is at the moment he was stopped. Let me check the story again. The story says: "He slowed down when he came to the main road, looked both ways to see that nothing was coming, and then drove across without stopping completely. At once he heard a police whistle, so he pulled into the side of the road and stopped." So when he heard the whistle, he was already driving across (onto the main road) and then pulled over. The policeman approached him after he had driven across. So at the moment he was stopped, he was driving on the main road? Or was he still entering? But the options are B) driving onto the main road. So when the police stopped him, he was in the process of driving
